items.headline: Senegal, Koulibaly in disarray as World Cup 'curse' for Africa's champions continues
items.description: As Senegal are learning to their dismay at the ongoing FIFA World Cup, Africa's champions have often struggled to recreate their continental dominance on the grandest stage.

items.headline: Mohamed Salah finally writes World Cup history with Egypt - can he salvage his international legacy?
items.description: Mohamed Salah has achieved more in the sport than any other Egyptian player in history, gracing the upper echelons of the game with Liverpool, winning silverware aplenty at Anfield, and establishing himself as a global superstar.

items.headline: Neymar: 'Really nervous' on Brazil return after 981 days
items.description: Neymar admitted he was "really nervous" when he ran onto the field and played for Brazil for the first time in 981 days.

items.headline: Aubrey Modiba: Canada must beware of Bafana 'upset' despite 'home' advantage
items.description: South Africa's Aubrey Modiba is quietly confident that Bafana Bafana can add to the World Cup's growing list of surprise results when they face Canada in their Last 32 match in Inglewood on Sunday.

items.headline: Jürgen Klopp shrugs off question about Bastian Schweinsteiger's remarks on Ivory Coast's play style
items.description: Jürgen Klopp broke off an interview after being asked about former Germany player Bastian Schweinsteiger's characterisations about the Ivory Coast's team's style of play.

items.headline: Anti-gay chant makes a return in Mexico's World Cup match against Czechia
items.description: Fans at Mexico's final World Cup group stage match on Wednesday chanted a slur that has previously led to fines and other sanctions against the country's soccer federation.

items.headline: No Bafana, no party! South Africa stun South Korea to advance to FIFA World Cup R32
items.description: South Africa, against all expectations, pulled off a 1-0 win against South Korea in Monterrey on Wednesday, gathering all three points they needed to advance to the round of 32 at the FIFA World Cup.

items.headline: Aguirre urges Mexico to stay grounded after perfect World Cup group stage
items.description: Mexico coach Javier Aguirre urged his team to keep moving 'step by step' after a 3-0 victory over Czechia sealed El Tri's first-ever perfect World Cup group stage.

items.headline: Aguirre's pragmatic Mexico has its doubters, but is built for World Cup success
items.description: Don't let the 3-0 scoreline fool you -- Mexico's win over Czechia was as pragmatic as they come, which is just how head coach Javier Aguirre had planned it.

items.headline: South Korea on brink of WC exit after 'wrong' Son gamble
items.description: South Korea coach Hong Myung-Bo had jaws dropping when he chose to leave out captain Son Heung-Min from his first XI for Wednesday's Group A decider against South Africa -- the first time since 2010 Son had not started a World Cup game.

items.headline: Relebohile Mofokeng proves he's made for the main stage in Bafana's big win

items.headline: Germany's Deniz Undav 'happy' in super-sub role at World Cup
items.description: Deniz Undav has three goals and two assists in 69 minutes at the World Cup, averaging a goal contribution every 13.8 minutes for a rate topping Lionel Messi (37.4), Kylian Mbappé (48.8) and Erling Haaland (51.2).
